criteria.description: A new module session_state.py exists as a top-level sibling of bot.py/wiki.py/documents.py (the Voice Tutor project root — the directory from which `import documents` already resolves and from which pytest is discovered), importable as `import session_state` from that root. It contains ONLY the pure, Pipecat-free session-state helper functions moved verbatim from git-HEAD bot.py, PLUS the Pipecat-free module-level constants/bindings those helpers transitively require UPSTREAM (the Path constants and their transitive dependencies), PLUS only the Pipecat-free import statements they need (copied from bot.py). It contains NO content from wiki.py or documents.py, no reference to the `wiki` or `documents` modules, no STT/TTS/Pipecat pipeline code, no LLM-backed/recap functions (generate_session_summary, generate_session_analysis, generate_artifact), no build_system_instruction, and NO non-moved bindings such as WIKI_ENABLED or downstream consumer constants (ARTIFACTS_DIR, SESSION_ANALYSIS_DIR, COST_LOG_PATH, COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH). Importing session_state has NO top-level side effects and, critically, requires NONE of the Pipecat/STT/TTS/pipeline/LLM/recap/wiki/documents stack to be installed or stubbed — it imports cleanly in a bare interpreter with only stdlib available.
criteria.verifyBy: In a FRESH subprocess whose sys.modules contains no pre-injected stubs, run `import session_state`; assert it succeeds AND assert immediately after that none of these are present in sys.modules: any name starting with 'pipecat', 'wiki', 'documents', 'anthropic'. Grep session_state.py source to confirm at module scope there is NO import of, or reference to: any pipecat/pipecat-submodule name, any STT/TTS/pipeline symbol, any recap/LLM function, `wiki`, `documents`, `anthropic`, `asyncio`, `dotenv`/`load_dotenv`, `loguru`, and that the tokens WIKI_ENABLED/ARTIFACTS_DIR/SESSION_ANALYSIS_DIR/COST_LOG_PATH/COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH/build_system_instruction/UsageAccumulator/BaseObserver/generate_session_summary/generate_session_analysis/generate_artifact do not appear anywhere in session_state.py.

criteria.description: A committed, independently re-derivable manifest recorded in the test module (list literals MOVED_HELPERS and MOVED_CONSTANTS) records the exact names moved, derived from git-HEAD bot.py by this rule: a module-level def/class in HEAD bot.py QUALIFIES iff, following the module-level name-reference graph in the UPSTREAM (dependencies-of) direction only, it references NONE of the following directly or transitively through any bot.py module-level binding: (a) any pipecat/STT/TTS/pipeline symbol, (b) any LLM/recap function, (c) the `wiki` module or any wiki symbol, (d) the `documents` module or any documents symbol. The derivation walks ONLY dependencies of movers (upstream); it NEVER pulls in downstream consumers of a mover. Filesystem/OS I/O is NOT disqualifying; inter-helper calls among qualifying helpers are permitted; every qualifying pure module-level binding a mover references transitively UPSTREAM also moves. EVERY qualifying helper is moved (completeness) and NO non-qualifying function is moved (exclusivity). The GROUND-TRUTH expected sets are these exact hardcoded literals: EXPECTED_HELPERS = {load_profile, _format_memory_date, append_to_memory, load_memory, _format_session_time, _format_full_transcript_block, load_most_recent_transcript_block}; EXPECTED_CONSTANTS = {PROFILE_PATH, MEMORY_PATH, TRANSCRIPTS_DIR, VOICE_TUTOR_DIR}. Expected EXCLUDED (must NOT move, remain in bot.py unchanged): build_system_instruction (references wiki), WIKI_ENABLED, UsageAccumulator (Pipecat BaseObserver), generate_session_summary, generate_session_analysis, generate_artifact (LLM/recap), bot (pipeline), and downstream consumer constants ARTIFACTS_DIR (= VOICE_TUTOR_DIR/...), SESSION_ANALYSIS_DIR, COST_LOG_PATH, COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH.
criteria.verifyBy: A test re-derives the qualifying set from git-HEAD bot.py source (via `git show HEAD:bot.py`, AST + module-level reference-graph walk in the upstream/dependencies-of direction with the 4-part disqualification rule) and asserts (i) the derived helper set == EXPECTED_HELPERS (hardcoded literal set), (ii) the derived constant set == EXPECTED_CONSTANTS (hardcoded literal set), (iii) set(MOVED_HELPERS) == EXPECTED_HELPERS, (iv) set(MOVED_CONSTANTS) == EXPECTED_CONSTANTS — so both the derivation AND the manifest are independently pinned to ground truth and a loose/broken walker cannot self-certify. The test additionally asserts each expected-EXCLUDED name (build_system_instruction, WIKI_ENABLED, UsageAccumulator, generate_session_summary, generate_session_analysis, generate_artifact, bot, ARTIFACTS_DIR, SESSION_ANALYSIS_DIR, COST_LOG_PATH, COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH) is absent from both MOVED_HELPERS and MOVED_CONSTANTS AND absent from both EXPECTED sets.

criteria.description: VERBATIM MOVE: for each moved helper AND each moved required constant/binding, its definition span in session_state.py is byte-identical to its git-HEAD span in bot.py (top-level defs/assignments at zero dedent), AND the parsed AST nodes are structurally equal. Any rename, signature/default change, decorator/docstring edit, comment removal/addition (including inserting `# pragma: no cover`), body reflow, or logic edit inside a moved span is a failure.
criteria.verifyBy: A test extracts each moved definition's/assignment's source span from git-HEAD bot.py (via `git show HEAD:bot.py`) and from session_state.py and asserts byte-identical spans and ast.dump equality of the corresponding FunctionDef/ClassDef/Assign nodes.

criteria.description: bot.py re-imports the relocated names (helpers AND required constants) from session_state.py so its public surface is unchanged: every moved helper remains resolvable as bot.<name>, every moved constant remains resolvable as bot.<const>, and all pre-existing internal call sites (including excluded functions like bot()/generate_* that reference moved constants such as VOICE_TUTOR_DIR/TRANSCRIPTS_DIR) still reference the same unqualified names and continue to resolve. Because git-HEAD bot.py imports the Pipecat/STT/TTS/LLM stack at module top level and the verifier's .harness-venv deliberately does NOT install that stack, `import bot` is performed ONLY under the deterministic Pipecat-stub fixture defined in c12 (which injects lightweight stand-in modules into sys.modules for exactly the top-level third-party imports of bot.py that are not resolvable in the harness venv), so a correct verbatim move can PASS without installing pipecat.
criteria.verifyBy: Under the c12 stub fixture, import bot successfully, then assert hasattr(bot, name) for every name in MOVED_HELPERS and MOVED_CONSTANTS. Independently (without importing bot), verify via AST/source that working-tree bot.py contains exactly one `from session_state import (...)` statement whose imported names are exactly the union of MOVED_HELPERS and MOVED_CONSTANTS.

criteria.description: BOT.PY DIFF IS MECHANICAL: the diff of working-tree bot.py vs HEAD consists ONLY of (a) removal of the moved definition/assignment spans (helper defs AND required constant assignments) and (b) addition of exactly one `from session_state import (...)` statement importing exactly the union of MOVED_HELPERS and MOVED_CONSTANTS (no more, no fewer). PLACEMENT: that single re-import MUST be at a module-level position (indent 0, not inside any def/class/if/try) that lexically PRECEDES every retained module-level statement that consumes a moved constant — in particular it MUST precede the retained `ARTIFACTS_DIR = VOICE_TUTOR_DIR / "artifacts"` assignment — so import-time execution order resolves the moved constants. ORDER-PRESERVATION INVARIANT: the relative lexical order of ALL retained top-level statements (every top-level statement not in a moved span) MUST be identical to their order in HEAD bot.py; no retained statement — notably WIKI_ENABLED and the interleaved constant block containing ARTIFACTS_DIR/SESSION_ANALYSIS_DIR/COST_LOG_PATH/COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH — may be relocated, reordered, merged, or split. Non-moved bindings physically interleaved among moved spans MUST remain byte-identical, contributing no diff beyond their possible change of neighboring blank lines. Pipecat-free imports copied into session_state.py also stay in bot.py where still needed (contribute no diff). No edits to STT/TTS/Pipecat pipeline code, LLM/recap functions, build_system_instruction, WIKI_ENABLED, or any non-moved binding. BLANK-LINE NORMALIZATION (exact, applied identically to BOTH the reconstructed-expected text and the working-tree bot.py text before comparison): (1) rstrip every line; (2) collapse every run of ≥2 consecutive blank lines into exactly ONE blank line; (3) strip all leading and trailing blank lines at file edges; (4) ensure exactly one trailing newline. No other whitespace edits are permitted inside retained content.
criteria.verifyBy: A test reconstructs expected bot.py from HEAD by deleting ONLY the moved helper AND constant spans (leaving every interleaved non-moved binding such as WIKI_ENABLED in place and in original order) and inserting the single re-import of MOVED_HELPERS+MOVED_CONSTANTS at the top-level import region above the first retained moved-constant consumer, applies the exact 4-step normalization to BOTH texts, and asserts equality. The test additionally asserts: (i) WIKI_ENABLED and ARTIFACTS_DIR/SESSION_ANALYSIS_DIR/COST_LOG_PATH/COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH assignment spans are byte-identical in working-tree bot.py vs HEAD; (ii) ORDER-PRESERVATION: the ordered sequence of retained top-level statement identities (by AST node key / normalized source) in working-tree bot.py equals the HEAD sequence with moved spans removed and the single import inserted at the pinned location — proving no retained statement was reordered; (iii) via AST, the inserted `from session_state import` node is at module level and its lineno is strictly less than the lineno of the retained `ARTIFACTS_DIR = VOICE_TUTOR_DIR / "artifacts"` assignment and of every other retained module-level statement referencing a name in MOVED_CONSTANTS.

criteria.description: RE-EXPORT IDENTITY: for each manifest name (helper or constant), getattr(bot, name) is getattr(session_state, name) — bot exposes the very same object (function or Path constant) defined in session_state.py, not a copy or wrapper. This is verified with bot imported under the c12 Pipecat-stub fixture (since bot is unimportable in the bare harness venv); the stub fixture does not shadow, replace, or re-wrap session_state or any moved name — it stubs ONLY the absent third-party stack — so the identity assertion reflects the real re-export.
criteria.verifyBy: Under the c12 stub fixture, import both bot and session_state and assert object identity (`is`) between bot.<name> and session_state.<name> for every name in MOVED_HELPERS and MOVED_CONSTANTS. The test also asserts session_state is NOT among the modules stubbed by c12 (i.e. it was imported for real), guaranteeing the identity is genuine.

criteria.description: IMPORT SAFETY and FREE-VARIABLE resolution ON BOTH MODULES. (A) session_state: `import session_state` succeeds with NO stub fixture active (bare stdlib env); no moved helper has unresolved free/global names — every module-level binding it references was moved and every imported name it needs is satisfied by a copied import in session_state.py. (B) bot: under the c12 Pipecat-stub fixture, `import bot` succeeds; POST-EDIT bot.py's retained code that referenced a moved constant still resolves those names via the re-import — no bot.py free name is left dangling by the extraction. (C) IMPORT-TIME EXECUTION ORDER: during `import bot` (under c12), the retained top-level assignment `ARTIFACTS_DIR = VOICE_TUTOR_DIR / "artifacts"` resolves VOICE_TUTOR_DIR (imported from session_state) without NameError because the `from session_state import (...)` re-import runs first, and bot.ARTIFACTS_DIR equals session_state.VOICE_TUTOR_DIR / "artifacts". Retained excluded functions/constants that consume moved constants (generate_*/bot() using TRANSCRIPTS_DIR/VOICE_TUTOR_DIR) resolve them via the re-import. No moved helper silently depends on a bot.py global left behind, and no retained bot.py binding silently loses a moved constant.
criteria.verifyBy: (A) In a fresh subprocess with no stubs, import session_state; a test compiles session_state.py and checks each moved helper's global free names resolve within session_state's namespace (no NameError when invoked on CASES inputs). (B/C) A test compiles working-tree bot.py and, via module-level free-name analysis, asserts every module-level name referenced by any retained bot.py binding is resolvable in bot's namespace after the re-import (each moved constant used by retained code — TRANSCRIPTS_DIR, VOICE_TUTOR_DIR via ARTIFACTS_DIR — is importable and resolves). Under the c12 stub fixture, import bot and assert bot.ARTIFACTS_DIR == session_state.VOICE_TUTOR_DIR / "artifacts", proving import-time order resolved VOICE_TUTOR_DIR for the retained assignment.

criteria.description: A single committed input corpus CASES under tests/ maps each MOVED_HELPERS name to a list of concrete non-trivial argument tuples (representative + edge/error cases) and is the ONLY input source for c8–c10. For EVERY manifest helper at least one test asserts its exact present-day return value on a representative CASES input, pinning a concrete literal/data structure. Filesystem-touching helpers (load_profile, append_to_memory, load_memory, load_most_recent_transcript_block, and any transitively FS-dependent formatter) are exercised under a HERMETIC tmp-dir fixture that monkeypatches session_state's module-level Path constants (PROFILE_PATH/MEMORY_PATH/TRANSCRIPTS_DIR/VOICE_TUTOR_DIR) to a tmp_path; the real ~/.voice-tutor directory MUST NOT be created or mutated (a snapshot guard asserts the production dir is untouched). DETERMINISM: for the locale-sensitive formatters _format_memory_date and _format_session_time (strftime with %B / AM-PM), each characterization assertion pins a FIXED expected string and the suite sets/asserts a deterministic locale (C or en_US.UTF-8) via a fixture (locale.setlocale, or explicit skip/xfail guard if unavailable) so month-name/period assertions are not environment-flaky. Tautological (f(x)==f(x)), truthiness-only, or self-comparison assertions are prohibited; expected values are hardcoded literals, not re-derived by calling the helper or re-invoking strftime in the test.
criteria.verifyBy: Run pytest; confirm each manifest helper has a characterization test asserting a hardcoded literal against a CASES input; inspect that FS helpers use a tmp-dir fixture redirecting the session_state constants and that a guard asserts the real ~/.voice-tutor path is untouched; confirm the formatter tests pin fixed month-name/AM-PM literals under an explicit deterministic-locale fixture; confirm assertions compare to concrete literals (not the function's own output or a live strftime call).

criteria.description: Branch/edge/error coverage over the shared corpus with NO pragma escape on reachable branches. For every manifest helper the tests exercise each reachable branch/return path via CASES inputs (using the hermetic tmp-dir fixture from c8), asserting a concrete current output literal or pytest.raises on the EXACT current exception type; message matching is verbatim only for static-literal messages, else a stable substring/regex match. REQUIRED branch pairs, each exercised with concrete-literal or pytest.raises assertions and NO `# pragma: no cover`: (1) load_profile: PROFILE_PATH exists → file content vs. absent → ""; (2) load_memory: MEMORY_PATH absent → "" vs. present → content; (3) append_to_memory: MEMORY_PATH absent → header/file-created branch (assert seeded header IS written) vs. present → append-only branch (assert header NOT re-written, new text appended); (4) load_most_recent_transcript_block: TRANSCRIPTS_DIR missing → None; present but no eligible files (only .usage.json / empty) → None; populated → correct most-recent formatted block, asserting both the .usage.json exclusion filter and the reverse-sort most-recent selection; (5) _format_memory_date and (6) _format_session_time: assert AM/PM and the noon/midnight boundary of `hour % 12 or 12` with fixed expected strings for 00:xx (midnight → 12 AM) and 12:xx (noon → 12 PM). A `# pragma: no cover` is permitted ONLY for a branch the test author names AND proves unreachable; no such pragma may appear inside a moved span in session_state.py; pragmas live ONLY in test files or coverage config.
criteria.verifyBy: Run pytest with branch coverage MEASURED SPECIFICALLY on session_state.py restricted to the moved-helper source-span line ranges (computed from the c3 spans), DRIVEN by the CASES corpus, and assert 100% of those spans' reachable branches/lines are covered. The coverage assertion MUST FAIL (not skip/xfail) if any manifest helper's span is absent from the coverage report or below 100%. Explicitly confirm each required branch pair (1)–(6) is present with a concrete-literal or pytest.raises assertion (not skipped, not pragma-suppressed); confirm exception cases use pytest.raises on exact types; confirm no `# pragma` appears within any moved span in session_state.py; assert any pragma outside a moved span carries a named unreachability justification and covers none of the required branch pairs.

criteria.description: DUAL-IMPORT INTERFACE PROOF: for every manifest helper the tests invoke it through BOTH bot.<helper> and session_state.<helper> on EVERY CASES input and assert equal results (or identical raised exception type/message), proving the relocation preserved behavior across both import paths. bot is imported under the c12 Pipecat-stub fixture (the harness venv lacks the real stack). The hermetic tmp-dir fixture patches session_state's module-level constants — the namespace the moved functions close over — so both import paths observe the patched constants; the c12 stubs do NOT patch or shadow session_state, so bot.<helper> and session_state.<helper> are the same object (per c6) and read the same patched globals.
criteria.verifyBy: A parametrized test over CASES, run under the c12 stub fixture, calls both bot.<helper> and session_state.<helper> and asserts equality of returns or matching raised exception type/message for every case; the tmp-dir fixture patches session_state.<CONST> and the test confirms both import paths observe the patched value (demonstrating the moved functions read session_state's globals regardless of import path).

criteria.description: The full test suite is discoverable and passes from the project root (dir containing bot.py) with zero failures/errors, and the ONLY production changes are the bot.py→session_state.py relocation (moved helper defs + moved required constants) plus bot.py's single re-import (c5). Permitted non-production additions: test files under tests/ (CASES corpus, the c12 stub-fixture conftest scaffolding, optional advisory golden), minimal tests/__init__.py/conftest.py scaffolding if required, and an optional coverage config. wiki.py, documents.py, app.py, the STT/TTS/Pipecat pipeline, LLM/recap functions, and ALL non-moved module-level bindings in bot.py are untouched (byte-identical to HEAD). The prior sprint's documents.py characterization tests (if present) are not deleted, skipped, weakened, or reordered and still pass. Explicitly, EVERY module-level binding in HEAD bot.py NOT in MOVED_HELPERS∪MOVED_CONSTANTS must have a byte-identical span in working-tree bot.py, including WIKI_ENABLED, ARTIFACTS_DIR, SESSION_ANALYSIS_DIR, COST_LOG_PATH, COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH, MIN_ANALYSIS_DURATION_SEC, MIN_SUMMARY_DURATION_SEC, and every PRICE_* constant. Any behavioral GOLDEN baseline over CASES is regenerated by the verifier by exec'ing the extracted HEAD source spans of the manifest helpers+required bindings+copied imports in an ISOLATED namespace — NEVER by importing whole HEAD bot.py (which is unimportable without the stack) — and the post-move session_state helpers must reproduce it identically.
criteria.verifyBy: Run `pytest` from project root → zero failures/errors; `git diff --name-only HEAD` shows only session_state.py, bot.py, and tests/ additions (plus optional coverage config); confirm wiki.py, documents.py, app.py are byte-identical to HEAD (git diff empty). A test enumerates ALL module-level binding names in HEAD bot.py (via AST on `git show HEAD:bot.py`) and, for every name NOT in MOVED_HELPERS∪MOVED_CONSTANTS, asserts its span is byte-identical between HEAD and working-tree bot.py — explicitly including WIKI_ENABLED, ARTIFACTS_DIR, SESSION_ANALYSIS_DIR, COST_LOG_PATH, COST_LOG_JSONL_PATH, MIN_ANALYSIS_DURATION_SEC, MIN_SUMMARY_DURATION_SEC, and each PRICE_* constant. If a golden baseline exists, a test regenerates it by exec'ing extracted HEAD spans in an isolated namespace and asserts session_state helpers reproduce it on every CASES input.

criteria.description: DETERMINISTIC PIPECAT-STUB FIXTURE enabling `import bot` under the Pipecat-free .harness-venv so a correct verbatim move can be VERIFIED (not merely skipped). A pytest fixture (in tests/conftest.py or the test module) installs, into sys.modules BEFORE any `import bot`, lightweight stand-in modules for EXACTLY the set of bot.py top-level third-party imports that fail to resolve in the current environment — the stub set is DERIVED, not hardcoded: the fixture attempts to import each top-level third-party module bot.py imports (parsed from working-tree bot.py's import AST, excluding stdlib and the project-local modules session_state/wiki/documents) and stubs ONLY those raising ModuleNotFoundError, creating ModuleType placeholders (including required submodule paths and any attribute names bot.py imports from them, e.g. a BaseObserver base class that is a trivial object subclass) sufficient for bot's module body to execute. The fixture MUST NOT stub, shadow, replace, or re-wrap session_state, wiki, documents, or any stdlib module, and MUST remove its injected stubs on teardown (restoring sys.modules) so session_state's stub-free import (c1/c7A) and cross-test isolation are preserved. If bot.py has NO unresolvable third-party imports in the environment, the fixture is a no-op and bot imports natively. The stubs are inert placeholders that make no behavioral claim about the real stack; they exist solely so bot's module-level code (the re-import and retained constant assignments) can execute for the re-export/identity/dual-import proofs.
criteria.verifyBy: A test asserts: (i) the fixture derives its stub set from working-tree bot.py's import AST and stubs only currently-unresolvable third-party top-level modules (session_state/wiki/documents/stdlib never stubbed); (ii) under the fixture, `import bot` succeeds and bot.__file__ points at the working-tree bot.py; (iii) session_state is NOT in the fixture's stub set and is imported for real (used by c6 identity); (iv) after fixture teardown, sys.modules no longer contains the injected stub names, and a subsequent bare `import session_state` in a fresh subprocess still succeeds with zero stubs (c1); (v) the fixture performs no sys.path shadowing of production modules and no autouse mutation of session_state/bot behavior.
